description: Universal media converter for reading, filtering, and transcoding audio/video streams

arguments.name: Input file URL
arguments.description: Input file URL (can be a file, pipe, network stream, or grabbing device)

arguments.name: Output file URL
arguments.description: Output file URL. Anything on the command line that cannot be interpreted as an option is considered an output URL

arguments.name: Force format
arguments.description: Force input or output file format. Normally auto detected for input and guessed from extension for output

arguments.name: Codec
arguments.description: Select encoder (before output) or decoder (before input) for streams. Use stream specifier like -c:v, -c:a, -c:s. Use 'copy' for streamcopy

arguments.name: Video codec
arguments.description: Set the video codec. Alias for -codec:v

arguments.name: Audio codec
arguments.description: Set the audio codec. Alias for -codec:a

arguments.name: Subtitle codec
arguments.description: Set the subtitle codec. Alias for -codec:s

arguments.name: Map streams
arguments.description: Create one or more streams in the output file. Format: [-]input_file_id[:stream_specifier][:?] or [linklabel]

arguments.name: Duration
arguments.description: Limit duration of data read (input) or stop writing after duration (output). Mutually exclusive with -to, -t has priority

arguments.name: Stop at position
arguments.description: Stop writing the output or reading the input at position. Mutually exclusive with -t

arguments.name: Seek position
arguments.description: Seek to position in input (before -i) or discard output until position (before output)

arguments.name: Seek from EOF
arguments.description: Like -ss but relative to end of file. Negative values are earlier in the file, 0 is at EOF

arguments.name: File size limit
arguments.description: Set the file size limit in bytes. No further chunk of bytes is written after the limit is exceeded

arguments.name: Video filter
arguments.description: Create a video filtergraph and use it to filter the stream. Alias for -filter:v

arguments.name: Audio filter
arguments.description: Create an audio filtergraph and use it to filter the stream. Alias for -filter:a

arguments.name: Complex filtergraph
arguments.description: Define a complex filtergraph with arbitrary number of inputs and/or outputs. This is a global option

arguments.name: Video bitrate
arguments.description: Set video bitrate

arguments.name: Audio bitrate
arguments.description: Set audio bitrate

arguments.name: Frame rate
arguments.description: Set frame rate (Hz value, fraction, or abbreviation). As input, assumes constant frame rate. As output, duplicates or drops frames

arguments.name: Frame size
arguments.description: Set frame size. As output, inserts a scale video filter. Format is WxH

arguments.name: Aspect ratio
arguments.description: Set the video display aspect ratio. Can be floating point or num:den format

arguments.name: Pixel format
arguments.description: Set pixel format. Prefix with + to exit on error if format cannot be selected

arguments.name: Audio sample rate
arguments.description: Set the audio sampling frequency in Hz. Defaults to the frequency of the corresponding input stream

arguments.name: Audio channels
arguments.description: Set the number of audio channels. Defaults to the number of input audio channels

arguments.name: Audio sample format
arguments.description: Set the audio sample format. Use -sample_fmts to get a list of supported formats

arguments.name: Channel layout
arguments.description: Set the audio channel layout

arguments.name: Disable video
arguments.description: As input, blocks all video streams. As output, disables video recording

arguments.name: Disable audio
arguments.description: As input, blocks all audio streams. As output, disables audio recording

arguments.name: Disable subtitles
arguments.description: As input, blocks all subtitle streams. As output, disables subtitle recording

arguments.name: Disable data
arguments.description: As input, blocks all data streams. As output, disables data recording

arguments.name: Overwrite output
arguments.description: Overwrite output files without asking

arguments.name: Never overwrite
arguments.description: Do not overwrite output files and exit immediately if a specified output file already exists

arguments.name: Stream loop
arguments.description: Set number of times input stream shall be looped. 0 means no loop, -1 means infinite loop

arguments.name: Metadata
arguments.description: Set a metadata key/value pair. Can use metadata_specifier for streams, chapters, or programs

arguments.name: Disposition
arguments.description: Set disposition flags for a stream. Use + to add, - to remove, or 0 to clear. Use stream specifier like -disposition:a:1

arguments.name: Target type
arguments.description: Specify target file type. May be prefixed with pal-, ntsc-, or film-. Sets format options automatically

arguments.name: Frames to output
arguments.description: Stop writing to the stream after framecount frames. Use with stream specifier like -frames:v

arguments.name: Quality scale
arguments.description: Use fixed quality scale (VBR). Codec-dependent. Without stream specifier applies to video only

arguments.name: Encoding pass
arguments.description: Select the pass number for two-pass video encoding

arguments.name: Pass log file prefix
arguments.description: Set two-pass log file name prefix. Default is 'ffmpeg2pass'

arguments.name: Max frame rate
arguments.description: Set maximum output frame rate. Clamps output when auto-set and higher than this value. Cannot be used with -r

arguments.name: Display rotation
arguments.description: Set video rotation metadata in degrees counter-clockwise. Overrides file metadata

arguments.name: Display horizontal flip
arguments.description: Set whether on display the image should be horizontally flipped

arguments.name: Display vertical flip
arguments.description: Set whether on display the image should be vertically flipped

arguments.name: Auto rotate
arguments.description: Automatically rotate the video according to file metadata. Enabled by default, use -noautorotate to disable

arguments.name: Auto scale
arguments.description: Automatically scale the video according to the resolution of first frame. Enabled by default

arguments.name: Log level
arguments.description: Set logging level and flags. Optional flags prefix: repeat, level, time, datetime

arguments.name: Report
arguments.description: Dump full command line and log output to a file. Implies -loglevel debug

arguments.name: Hide banner
arguments.description: Suppress printing the copyright notice, build options, and library versions

arguments.name: Stats
arguments.description: Log encoding progress/statistics. On by default, use -nostats to disable

arguments.name: Stats period
arguments.description: Set period at which encoding progress/statistics are updated

arguments.name: Progress URL
arguments.description: Send program-friendly progress information to URL

arguments.name: Timestamp
arguments.description: Set the recording timestamp in the container

arguments.name: Recast media
arguments.description: Allow forcing a decoder of a different media type than the one detected by the demuxer

arguments.name: Input time offset
arguments.description: Set the input time offset. Positive offset means streams are delayed

arguments.name: Input timestamp scale
arguments.description: Rescale input timestamps by the given floating point scale value

arguments.name: Map metadata
arguments.description: Set metadata of next output file from infile. Supports specifiers g (global), s (stream), c (chapter), p (program)

arguments.name: Map chapters
arguments.description: Copy chapters from input file with given index to the next output file. Use negative index to disable

arguments.name: Shortest
arguments.description: Finish encoding when the shortest output stream ends

arguments.name: Shortest buffer duration
arguments.description: Maximum duration of buffered frames in seconds for -shortest option

arguments.name: Copy timestamps
arguments.description: Do not process input timestamps, keep their values without sanitization

arguments.name: Start at zero
arguments.description: When used with copyts, shift input timestamps so they start at zero

arguments.name: Bitexact
arguments.description: Enable bitexact mode for (de)muxer and (de/en)coder

arguments.name: Framerate mode
arguments.description: Set video framerate mode per output stream. Overrides -vsync

arguments.name: Force key frames
arguments.description: Force keyframes at specified times, expression, or source. Use stream specifier for per-stream

arguments.name: Encoder timebase
arguments.description: Set the encoder timebase. Can be 0 (default by media type), demux, filter, or a positive number

arguments.name: Bitstream filter
arguments.description: Apply bitstream filters to matching streams. Use stream specifier like -bsf:v. Comma-separated list

arguments.name: Tag/FourCC
arguments.description: Force a tag/fourcc for matching streams. Use stream specifier like -tag:v

arguments.name: Timecode
arguments.description: Specify timecode for writing. SEP is ':' for non-drop and ';' or '.' for drop timecode

arguments.name: Preset
arguments.description: Specify the preset for matching stream(s)

arguments.name: Attach file
arguments.description: Add an attachment to the output file (e.g. fonts for subtitle rendering in Matroska)

arguments.name: Dump attachment
arguments.description: Extract matching attachment stream into a file. Empty string uses filename metadata tag

arguments.name: Hardware acceleration
arguments.description: Use hardware acceleration to decode matching stream(s)

arguments.name: Hardware acceleration device
arguments.description: Select a device to use for hardware acceleration. Only makes sense with -hwaccel

arguments.name: Init hardware device
arguments.description: Initialize a new hardware device of given type, optionally with name and device parameters

arguments.name: Filter hardware device
arguments.description: Pass the named hardware device to all filters in any filter graph

arguments.name: Thread queue size
arguments.description: For input, max queued packets when reading. For output, max packets queued per muxing thread

arguments.name: Filter threads
arguments.description: Number of threads used to process a filter pipeline. Default is number of available CPUs

arguments.name: Read rate
arguments.description: Limit input read speed. Value 1 represents real-time speed. Default 0 means no limit

arguments.name: Real-time input
arguments.description: Read input at native frame rate. Equivalent to -readrate 1

arguments.name: Accurate seek
arguments.description: Enable accurate seeking in input files with -ss. Enabled by default, use -noaccurate_seek to disable

arguments.name: Ignore unknown streams
arguments.description: Ignore input streams with unknown type instead of failing if copying is attempted

arguments.name: Copy unknown streams
arguments.description: Allow input streams with unknown type to be copied instead of failing

arguments.name: Discard
arguments.description: Allows discarding specific streams or frames from streams

arguments.name: Apply cropping
arguments.description: Automatically crop the video after decoding according to file metadata

arguments.name: Stream ID
arguments.description: Assign a new stream-id value to an output stream. Format: output-stream-index:new-value

arguments.name: Max muxing queue size
arguments.description: Set the size of the muxing buffer in packets for the matching output stream

arguments.name: Muxing queue data threshold
arguments.description: Minimum threshold until which muxing queue size is not taken into account. Defaults to 50 megabytes per stream

arguments.name: Mux delay
arguments.description: Set the maximum demux-decode delay in seconds

arguments.name: Mux preload
arguments.description: Set the initial demux-decode delay in seconds

arguments.name: DTS delta threshold
arguments.description: Timestamp discontinuity delta threshold in seconds

arguments.name: DTS error threshold
arguments.description: Timestamp error delta threshold in seconds

arguments.name: Abort on
arguments.description: Stop and abort on various conditions

arguments.name: Max error rate
arguments.description: Set fraction of decoding frame failures across all inputs for exit code 69. Range 0 to 1

arguments.name: Stop on error
arguments.description: Stop and exit on error

arguments.name: Debug timestamps
arguments.description: Print timestamp/latency information. Mostly for testing and debugging

arguments.name: Benchmark
arguments.description: Show benchmarking information at the end of an encode

arguments.name: Benchmark all
arguments.description: Show benchmarking information during the encode for various steps

arguments.name: Time limit
arguments.description: Exit after ffmpeg has been running for specified duration in CPU user time seconds

arguments.name: Dump packets
arguments.description: Dump each input packet to stderr

arguments.name: Hex dump
arguments.description: When dumping packets, also dump the payload in hex

arguments.name: Reinit filter
arguments.description: Whether filtergraph is reinitialized when input frame parameters change mid-stream. Default enabled (1)

arguments.name: Drop changed frames
arguments.description: Drop frames with differing parameters mid-stream instead of reinitializing filtergraph. Default false (0)

arguments.name: SDP file
arguments.description: Print SDP information for an output stream to file

arguments.name: Pad audio output
arguments.description: Pad the output audio stream(s). Same as -af apad. Requires -shortest to take effect

arguments.name: Frame drop threshold
arguments.description: Frame drop threshold in frame rate units. Default is -1.1

arguments.name: Copy timebase
arguments.description: How to set encoder timebase when stream copying. 1=demuxer, 0=decoder, -1=auto

arguments.name: Copy initial non-keyframes
arguments.description: When doing stream copy, copy also non-key frames found at the beginning

arguments.name: Video stats
arguments.description: Dump video coding statistics to vstats_HHMMSS.log

arguments.name: Video stats file
arguments.description: Dump video coding statistics to the specified file

arguments.name: Fix subtitle duration
arguments.description: Fix subtitle durations by waiting for the next packet to avoid overlap

arguments.name: Canvas size
arguments.description: Set the size of the canvas used to render subtitles

arguments.name: Guess layout max
arguments.description: If input channel layout is unknown, try to guess only if it corresponds to at most this many channels. 0 to disable

arguments.name: SWS flags
arguments.description: Set default flags for the libswscale library used by auto-inserted scale filters

arguments.name: Rate control override
arguments.description: Rate control override for specific intervals. Format: int,int,int list separated by slashes

arguments.name: Print graphs
arguments.description: Print execution graph details to stderr

arguments.name: Print graphs file
arguments.description: Write execution graph details to the specified file

arguments.name: Print graphs format
arguments.description: Set the output format for graph details

arguments.name: Auto conversion filters
arguments.description: Enable auto-inserting format conversion filters in all filter graphs. On by default

arguments.name: Bits per raw sample
arguments.description: Declare the number of bits per raw sample in the given output stream

arguments.name: Isync
arguments.description: Assign an input as a sync source by input index. -1 means no adjustment

arguments.name: Filter complex threads
arguments.description: Number of threads used to process filter_complex graphs. Default is number of available CPUs

arguments.name: Filter buffered frames
arguments.description: Maximum number of buffered frames in a filtergraph. 0 means no limit
